factions.name: The Grace-Sworn
factions.description: The paladin order that has kept Hartstone neutral for three generations. They serve no king and take no side — only the compact. Their numbers are dwindling and their resources thin, but their reputation is the only thing stopping Hartstone from becoming a battlefield.

factions.name: House Vespar
factions.description: A powerful northern noble house pressing its claim to Hartstone with lawyers and armed escorts, in that order. Lady Ivra Malt speaks for the house with silk gloves and iron intent. They believe Hartstone is historically theirs.

log.note: Vespar's delegation exceeds the twelve-guard compact limit by twenty-eight. The Grace-Sworn are counting swords. Aldric has not yet invoked the clause.
Consequence of The Underbrick (ally): Three shipments of weapons have moved through the east tunnels in two weeks — too many for street crime. Consistent with equipping a small private force.
Consequence of The Underbrick (ally): Underbrick attacks Grace-Sworn patrol — open confrontation.

factions.name: The Compact Guild
factions.description: Hartstone's merchant class has thrived precisely because the city is neutral — both kingdoms trade through it, pay its tariffs, and need its roads. The Guild will back whoever keeps the trade routes open. Right now that is the compact. Guildmaster Sena Cord has a price she has not yet named.

factions.name: The Kelrath Envoys
factions.description: The southern Confederation sends diplomats instead of armies — so far. Emissary Dax Fell has been in Hartstone six months before Vespar's delegation arrived. He knows where every magistrate eats dinner and which ones have debts.

factions.name: The Underbrick
factions.description: Hartstone's criminal network operates in tunnels below the east quarter. They have survived every political change by being useful to whoever is winning. Someone is paying them very well right now. Nobody knows for what. (DM-only)

log.note: Three shipments of weapons have moved through the east tunnels in two weeks — too many for street crime. Consistent with equipping a small private force.
A Grace-Sworn patrol is ambushed in the east tunnels — non-lethal, but disarmed and blindfolded for two hours. A message is left: 'Stop looking.'
